key_insight: AdsMind adds physics-grounded relaxation feedback to a multi-agent LLM search loop so it can self-correct adsorption-configuration guesses and reliably find low-energy catalyst surface structures.
engineering_relevance: This can cut expensive DFT search effort for heterogeneous catalysis by making MLFF-accelerated discovery more reliable, interpretable, and automation-ready.
method: Multi-agent LLM proposes adsorption configurations for heterogeneous catalyst surfaces.
MLFF relaxation feedback is used in a closed loop to correct bad initial guesses.
The system is evaluated across multiple LLM backends and benchmark datasets, with DFT spot-checks on representative cases.
evidence_signal: Across AA20 and OCD-GMAE62, AdsMind reports 100% and 98.8% success rates, uses only 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case on average, and DFT validation shows it fixes adsorption-energy sign errors seen in open-loop outputs.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and transferability of the MLFF relaxation signal, so failure cases may still arise when the force field is inaccurate or the surface chemistry is highly out-of-distribution.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
